# Goals

# Purpose

A comprehensive, user-friendly mobile and desktop application to manage characters for the 2024 edition of *Dungeons & Dragons* (D\&D), reducing manual tracking and calculations during gameplay. The app should simplify and enhance gameplay—not replace the roleplaying experience. It should be designed to be a neutral facilitator that removes manual overhead but flexible enough for a player to manually override values. It is assumed that the player will be rolling physical dice but the option to add a dice roller can be added in the future. 

Inspiration: 

* [https://charkeeper.org/](https://charkeeper.org/) \- Existing Codebase and inspiration  
* [https://mothershipcompanion.com/](https://mothershipcompanion.com/) \- UI / UX  
* [https://charsheet.derikbadman.com/](https://charsheet.derikbadman.com/)  
* [https://lckynmbrsvn.github.io/DnD-5e-Character-Sheet/](https://lckynmbrsvn.github.io/DnD-5e-Character-Sheet/)  
* [https://adventurerscodex.com/player-tools.html](https://adventurerscodex.com/player-tools.html)  
* [https://crobi.github.io/dnd5e-quickref/preview/quickref.html](https://crobi.github.io/dnd5e-quickref/preview/quickref.html) \- For actions etc on the combat tab (pre-2024)

# Design Goals and Universal Behaviors

Here is a list of overarching design goals 

- There should be the need for minimal screen switching which means  
  - All the things you need for RP \+ Skill challenges should be on the character screen   
  - All the things needed for combat should be on the combat screen  
  - There should be minimal clutter; if adding a value to a screen makes it hard to use then it’s okay to be in a separate tab (spells)   
  - The ability to add custom should be inline with what is being added and context aware  
- For invalid or rule-breaking choices, The app will display non-blocking warnings like:  
  - You do not meet the ability prerequisites to multiclass into Paladin (13 STR, 13 CHA required).  
  - These warnings will include a brief explanation and source (PHB or TLC),  
  - be dismissible or collapsible,  
  - never prevent the user from continuing.  
- We'll also make it easy to:  
  - Undo or revise the decision  
  - View a summary of all "rule warnings" on the character overview page for quick diagnostics  
- Will be helpful and unrestrictive  
  - offers smart suggestions based on rule logic (e.g., “You gain proficiency in two skills from this list…”),  
  - allows the user to override, skip, or manually assign any value,  
  - highlights missing or incomplete areas gently (e.g., “you haven’t selected your Fighting Style yet”).  
  - any field can be re-edited manually  
  - the app never blocks the player from progressing due to incomplete or "incorrect" setups  
  - the system remembers what was done via wizard vs. manual override for traceability/debugging.  
  - The system will keep a log of all actions made for both admin and each character

# App

# App Screens

This is the various screens and values covered by the app from a regular user perspective. It is broken into tabs with the exception of the login screen and when there is no characters active for a user

- Login to go to the character screen  
  - Show the last selected character  
  - Create a Character wizard if there is no character 

- ## Topbar

- ### Heroic Inspiration

  - Toggle on and off

- ### Level \#

  - Touching / clicking Level gives a breakdown of all classes (e.g., “Fighter 3 / Wizard 2”) with a class-level summary view, as the option to Level Up 

- ### Character Name

  - Truncate name if too long

- ## Character

  - ### Ability Scores

    - Option to edit values 

  - ### Proficiency Bonus

  - ### Size

  - ### Passive Perception

  - ### Saving Throws

    - Ability to edit to add modifiers

  - ### Skills

    - Skills, their score, and if that skill currently has advantage / disadvantage  
    - Ability to click on a skill to see Ability modifier, Proficiency Modifier, Any Active Effect modifier, What is giving Adv / Disadv, ability to edit the skill to give it a custom modifier  
    - Ability to manually add a proficiency   
    - Should account for effects / feats such as expertise, jack-of-all-trades  
    - Should account for magic items bonuses like “Quartz helm of Clarity” 

  - ### Other modifiers 

    - Species, Feat and Intrinsic notes that aren’t directly combat related  
    - that aren’t combat related

- ## Combat

  - ### Armor Class, Initiative, Speed

    - Speed should either have multiple smaller fields or one larger one to be able to breakdown walk/fly/swim/burrow  
    - Ability to edit for custom modifier

  - ### Spell DC

    - If some spell types have a higher or lower DC than regular there should be a star here   
    - Clicking it will show the DC of each spell type with the ability to add / remove modifiers

  - ### Health, Max Health, Temp Hitpoints, Hit Dice

    - Have an easy to toggle buttons to add or remove hitpoints  
      - Should be able to heal above max health  
    - Touching / clicking on the points should allow you to update the max health and temp health numbers

  - ### Death Saving Throw Success \+ Failures

  - ### Exhaustion Points

  - ### Concentration

    - Automatically toggles if you cast a spell with Concentration

  - ### Status Toggles

    - Show all the status Effects that can be acquired

  - ### Damage Resistances

    - Quickly see all currently active vulnerabilities, resistances and immunities  
    - Ability to click / touch section to toggle on or off different abilities

  - ### Spell Slots

    - Should have empty boxes that you can touch to fill

  - ### Actions

    - Any action that is available either via spell, rules (e.g. study action), Equipped Items, Class abilities, Consumable items or feats each broken into a subsection  
      - Spells should be shown by level in their section  
      - Each subsection should be collapsible to hide it if you don’t want to see it  
      - Feats that give you 1 free spell use should show the spell in the feats section for the use option and as a prepared spell in the spell section  
    - Touch / Clicking on the name of the skill or spell should pop up a description   
    - For actions that have them, the attack bonus or DC should be displayed (excluding Spells), the damage modifier and damage type or types  
    - For limited use actions it should a toggle to use a charge and  give you a count of how many times you can use it before a short / long rest  
      - ![][image1]  
    - If you use a spell it should give you a list of levels you can cast it at and clicking one will toggle a spell slot  
      - Spells should show the damage dice, DC and type if applicable  
      - Should default to consume an ability / feat free cast first if available e.g.("can cast X once without expending a spell slot")   
        - Include an override/toggle for powers/abilities (Use spell slot instead of ability)  
      - If Spell is available through item use should give a toggle to use item instead of Spell Slot (Wand of Magic Missles)   
      -   
    - Items that have charges should be indicated in the same was as limited actions  
    - Items that are consumable should be noted. When you use one it should decrease the inventory by that amount

  - ### Bonus Actions

    - Should behave the same as actions except for Bonus Actions

  - ### Reactions

    - Should behave the same as actions except for Reactions

- ## Items

  - ### Base categories

    - Equipped — affects stats (AC, movement, resistances, spell DCs, etc.),  
    - Backpack — does not affect stats   
      - Subsection of “Used” for consumables with a 0 qty  
    - Stored (e.g., bank, cart, home) \- does not affect stats and pushed to the bottom

  - ### Gold total

    - Number should just be gold with silver being .1 gold and copper being .01 gold from the rules

  - ### Add an Item

    - Option to add a mundane item, search for an item or add a custom item with the Item Wizard  
    - Custom items (user-created) can define:  
      - bonus types (flat, conditional),  
      - what they apply to (AC, skills, spells, etc.),  
      - if attunement is required.  
      - Any additional effect and what tab it should be displayed in (Combat, Non-Combat, Both)

  - ### Consumable Items

    - Have a QTY amount with a \+/- Toggle to modify  
    - When 0 QTY move to “Used” section in backpack  
    - 0 QTY items only show up under the items tab, not under any others 

  - ### Equipped Items

    - The system automatically applies its mechanical effects, including:  
      - Bonuses to AC, saving throws, ability checks,  
      - Resistances, immunities, movement speed changes,  
      - Spell save DC/attack bonuses (e.g., Rod of the Pact Keeper),  
      - Weapon and armor mastery effects, if applicable,  
      - Spellcasting bonuses  
    - When unequipped to backpack or storage it removes those bonuses  
    - Backpack \= Inactive (regardless of attunement status)  
    - When equipping an attunement item it goes into the attunement section rather than the equipped items  
      - All Attuned items are considered “Equipped”

  - #### Attuned Items

    - Default Limit for attuned items is 3 per character.  
      - Certain skills / abilities should be able to change this number  
      - When equipping an attuned item and have max attuned items equipped then you are prompted to choose which attuned item to replace which unequips it   
        - You have max attuned items. Please choose an item to unattune to:  
        - Allow override to ignore the limit (with a warning),  
        - Allow Equipping without attunement  
        - Equipped but Not Attuned \= Inactive (gray icon or label)  
        -   
  - Stored Items  
  - Spell Components  
    - Should this section have interaction with the Spells?   
  - Pre-populated item database \+ custom item creation  
    - Non-Magical weapons / Armor should have the ability to be edited to add basic “+X” enhancements. Any effect above that requires searching the database or adding a custom item  
    - Each item includes:  
      - Type, rarity, attunement requirement, charges, effects, weight, gold value.  
      - Affects: attack bonus, damage, saves, skills, speed, tools, languages, stats.  
    - Magic Items should be denoted by color  
      - Common \- Grey  
      - Uncommon \- Green  
      - Rare \- Blue  
      - Legandry \- Purple  
      - Unique \- Gold   
      - Astrix if requires attunement  
  - Consumable or Basic items have a Quantity input with quick-edit (+/- buttons),  
  - Weapons, Armor and Magic items have an Equip/Unequip toggle with immediate stat recalculation  
  - Charged Magic Item Behavior:  
    - Each item with charges (e.g., Wand of Fireballs, Staff of Healing) will:  
    - show current / max charges (e.g., 3 / 7),  
    - allow manual adjustment at any time,  
    - display the recharge rule (e.g., “regains 1d6+1 charges at dawn”),  
  - 

- ## Spells

- Each Section should be broken out by Class

  - ### Prepared Spells

    - Filters  
    - Always prepared section at bottom

  - ### Learned Spells

  - ### Ability or Item Spells

    - For preparing / learning / managing spells not casting them  
    - Should be able to filter by  
      - Level, components, cost, range, school, description, casting time, duration component (verbal/semantic/material), concentration, ritual  
  - Should be able to toggle on off the default display of  
    - Level (if not grouped by) components, cost, range, school (if not grouped by), description, casting time, duration component (verbal/semantic/material), concentration, ritual  
  - Should be able to group by  
    - Level, School  
  - Should have a section for each class you have if it has magic  
    - These sections should be collapsible  
  - Show Cantrip Max and total number of prepared spells  
    - Should be able to prepare more than these number of spells  
  - Each section should conform to the rules of each class  
    - Wizards should see their known spells, have the option to add spells to their spell book  
  - You should be able to Add a spell from another class in each class section with an override option  
    - For learned spell classes this will be a button on the learn spell toggle  
    - For prepared spell classes this will be a button next to the class name  
  - The system will still calculate the allowed number of prepared spells per class/ruleset (e.g., “You can prepare 5 Cleric spells”),  
    - Players will be able to prepare as many as they want,  
    - receive a soft warning if they exceed the limit:  
    - You have prepared 8 spells, but your limit is 5\. (Cleric Level 3 \+ WIS modifier 2\)  
  - Prepared spells will be manually selected/toggled per class,  
    - No automatic reset per day/session — players control prep  
    - a quick “Clear All” toggle,  
    - Spells gained from feats, items, or multiclassing will be grouped separately with prep toggles if needed.  
  - For Known Spell Classes The system will calculate and display how many spells the class is allowed to know at their level,  
    - Players will be able to add additional known spells anyway,  
    - When they exceed the RAW number the UI will show a soft warning:  
    - You know 10 Bard spells, but are only allowed 8 at level 6\.  
  - We'll allow players add or remove spells easily  
    - We will have a “Remove All” toggle for convenience

- ## Aptitudes

  - ### Feats

    - Shows a summary title (e.g., “Wyrmkin Adept”)  
    - Expands to list all granted elements (e.g., “Tool Proficiency: Tinker’s Tools, Language: Draconic”  
    - Grouped by  
      - Origin  
      - General   
      - Fighting Style  
      - Epic Boon  
      - Val'Ruvina

  - ### Languages

  - ### Weapons \+ Armor

    - #### Profecenies

      - If subset should have an option for picking specific types of weapons / armor

    - #### Mastery

      - Show count of weapon types you get mastery of  
      - Select Mastery  
        - Can select more than allowed with warning  
      - Weapons in their inventory that qualify will have mastery tags auto-applied  
      - Weapon attacks auto-update to reflect mastery effect on combat tab

  - ### Tools and Instruments

  - ### Intrinsics 

    - Semi-Permanent or Miscellaneous Effects, These are the Supernatural Gifts, curses, abilities, modifications that are granted over the course of the Leyfarers campaign  
    - Traits from your Lineage selection

- ## Rest

  - Toggles a pop up that has short rest, long rest and session rest

  - ### Short Rest

    - Refresher for short-rest abilities.  
    - Select hit dice used \+ HP recovered.  
    - Display list of refreshed features.  
    - Spell slot restoration  
    - Optional conditions  
    - Custom recharge items or charges  
    - Give option to swap abilities that permit it  
    - Give option to restore per session abilities (unchecked default)

  - ### Long Rest Action:

    - Fully restore HP.  
    - Remove 1 exhaustion point  
      - Can toggle to remove more  
    - Spell slot restoration   
    - Custom recharge items or charges  
    - Restore all abilities tied to short/long rest.  
    - Give option to swap abilities that permit it  
      - Weapon Masteries  
    - Optional conditions  
    - Give option to restore per session abilities (unchecked default)

  - ### Session Action

    - Restores Per session abilities;  
    - Prompted dialog box to take long rest / short rest (launches rest screen)

  - ### Rest screen:

    - Summarize what will refresh  
    - Allow the user to confirm before applying it  
    - Players can toggle individual refresh actions:  
      - Restore Rage Uses (2/2)  
      - Recharge Wand of the Warmage (currently 0/3)  
      - Regain 1 Exhaustion Level  
      - Restore Spell Slots  
      - Refresh “Flame Step” (Custom Intrinsic)  
    - “Apply Rest” button will  
      - Execute chosen recovery steps  
      - Leave others untouched.

- ## Settings

  - ### Character Settings

    - Change Character or create a new one  
    - Upload an avatar for your character  
      - Displayed on Select Character Menu  
    - Delete this Character  
      - Make sure that they confirm by typing “delete me”   
    - Reset All User Modifications / Overrides  
    - Show Avatar on Character Screen

  - ### Change Log

    - All changes made will be logged to the character change log   
    - Log entries will be batched to have of multiple changes (- 10 hit points, condition of fear)  
      - Batching will happen for all changes between last entry to after X seconds of inactivity  
    - Rest will log “Last Rest: Long, 08/04/25, \+1 HD used, 2 spells restored.”

  - ### Exporting

  - Format  
    - Official-Style Sheet Mimics the 2024 D\&D character sheet layout,  
    - App-Style Sheet Mirrors tabs from the app (Character, Combat, Spells, etc.)  
  - Select Sections  
    - Sections to include:  
    - Core Stats  
    - Combat Stats  
    - Spells  
    - Items & Equipment  
    - Aptitudes  
    - Companion Sheet  
  - Button to Select All, or Summary  
    - Summary is Core Stats \+ Combat Stats  
  - Option to include overrides and annotations (e.g., “AC is 18 due to Bracers of Defense \+ manual override”)  
  - Export Behavior:  
    - Single-click PDF download,  
    - Filename auto-generated as: CharacterName\_Level\_Class\_MM-DD-YYYY.pdf  
      - Ability to select the sections you want to export  
        - If you don’t want to export 

  - ### Share via live link

    - Real-time sync of changes with shared link  
    - Only 1 active link at a time  
    - Can copy/revoke links  
    - Content filters:  
      -  Share stats only  
      -  Share actions \+ spells only  
      -  Share inventory only  
      -  Share everything (default)

  - ### Account specific settings

    - Reset Password  
    - Update Email  
    - Feedback  
    - Links  
    - Logout

- ## Companion

  - Companions are familiars, etc. that have a limited subsection of the above attributes This system will cover warlock familiars, summoned companions, pets, and bonded creaturesCompanions are tied to an existing character on your account which creates a special "Companion" section in Combat and in character pages  
  - A special type of character that is tied to the companion list from the admin  
  - Creating a companion requires you to select a character it is tied to   
    - You can reassign the companion to a different character on your account on the companion character sheet   
    - You can assign multiple companions to a single character   
  - Companions do not level up, do not gain XP, and do not unlock new features automatically.  
  - Can Manually edit HP, AC, actions, or stats at any time,  
  - Can Create and assign new traits 

  - ### Companion Creation

    - Created via a **limited character sheet UI**, with:  
    - Name, type  
      - Type Should be a selectable list as defined in the admin   
    - Ability scores  
    - HP, AC, movement  
    - Actions / spells / traits

  - ### Inventory

    - Each companion has its own mini-inventory:  
      - Equipped/backpack only  
      - Supports consumables and attunement  
    - Items (e.g., potions, infused collars, spell batteries) can be used from the companion sheet or the combat tab of the main character

  - ### Combat Tab

    - Appears as a compact card in the player’s Combat tab, with  
      - HP  
      - Conditions (e.g., poisoned, unconscious),  
      - Initiative bonus  
      - Action buttons (e.g., bite, help, magic attack),  
    - Allow modifications in the same way a main character does

  - ### Character tab

    - On Character tab, players can press “Switch to Companion”:  
      - Swaps to the full editable companion sheet,  
      - “Return to Owner” button brings them back.

  - ### Rest & Recharge:

    - Follows same rest mechanics:  
    - Hit dice (if relevant),  
    - Short/long rest usage tracking,  
    - Custom features supported.

# Behaviors

# App Behaviors

- Display both system-calculated and user-overridden values where relevant  
- include small indicators (e.g., asterisk, pencil icon) to denote overrides  
- Dedicated Sections are collapsible for each tab  
- ensure all fields used in gameplay (HP, AC, spell slots, etc.) are editable inline by the player  
- allow undo/revert to calculated state where applicable  
- We’ll track per-use features under the actions tab in combat like:  
  - Rage (Barbarian),  
  - Wild Shape (Druid),  
  - Channel Divinity (Cleric),  
  - Action Surge / Second Wind (Fighter),  
  - Lay on Hands (Paladin),  
  - and similar abilities from TLC subclasses.  
  - Where applicable Each feature gets its own usage counter (e.g., 0 / 2),  
  - These counters will:  
    - auto-increment when the player takes a short or long rest,  
    - include a "manual adjust" toggle for overrides  
- During Long Rest or Session Rest:  
  - Rechargeable items will be included in the “Rest Summary” panel (e.g., “Wand of Magic Missiles: roll recharge?”),  
  - Have an increment or deduction buttons to manually adjust the amount of charges gained 

- ## Items

  - ### General

    - Magic item effects will be fully described in a tooltip or quick-expand view similar to how Spells work

  - ### Actions Tab (Combat UI)

    - Weapons, consumables, and limited-use items (like a Wand of Fireballs) will show:  
    - Attack roll bonus (with magic bonuses factored in),  
    - Damage type/dice,  
    - Save DC (if applicable),  
    - Usage limits with counters (e.g., “1 of 3 charges used”)

  - ### Spells Tab

    - Items that grant innate spellcasting (e.g., “cast *Levitate* once per long rest”) will:  
      - Appear under a “External Sources” spell section,  
      - Be filterable by source (Class, Feat, Item, etc.),  
      - Respect per-day/per-rest limits and track usage automatically,  
      - Include recharge logic (e.g., 1/day, regains d6/day).

  - ### Passive Effects

    - Gear that grants advantage/disadvantage, movement changes, skill bonuses, resistances, or conditions will:  
      - Auto-apply in background mechanics (e.g., auto-calculate stealth bonus),  
      - Be noted with a passive effect tag in the character summary and conditions panel  
    - Toggle off if the item is unequipped or unattuned.

  - ### Consumables

    - Consumable items like potions, scrolls, and bombs will:  
    - Appear in a “Consumables” subsection of the Actions or Combat tab,  
    - Auto-decrease quantity when used  
    - Move to “Used” bin or gray out when depleted in backpack when depleted

# Admin

# Admin

The goal of the admin is twofold; one is to get around the Hasbro Lawyer problem (we could use OGL data but…) and the second is to allow us to manage and add future unlocks from the Leyfarers universe. Looking more globally it allows anyone to selfhost a copy and add their own homebrew content. The interface should be similar to a spreadsheet with different sections for each type. This should also integrate with the wizard when more advanced things are needed.

- ## Anatomy of an effect

  - Feats, Spells, Magic Items, Intrinsics can effect a character in a number of ways  
    -   
-


- ## Content Management

  - ### General

    - Content Visibility States:  
      - Public; visible and searchable by all users  
      - Locked; can not be added to new characters, doesn’t show up in search but characters can still have it on their sheet  
      - Depreciated; can not be added to new characters and the entity is removed from existing characters prompting them to replace it  
        - User should get a warning on first time the character is opened about what has been removed from their sheet  
      - Hidden; can be included on characters but does not appear in selection menus or search unless you type in the exact name of the item

  - ### Feats

  - ### Skills

  - ### Proficiencies

  - ### Classes \+ Sub Classes

  - ### Spells

    - Admin defines spellcasting capability by class and allowable spells

  - ### Items; Magic and Mundane

    - 

    - #### Weapon and Armor Types

    - 

    - #### Tools and Instruments

    - 

  - ### Species and Lineages

  - ### Intrinsics

    - Can create companion specific intrinsics / Traits (Owl Flyby for example)  
    - Supernatural Gifts, Abilities, Modifications

  - ### Languages

  - ### 

  - ### Companions Templates

    - The various types of companions (e.g. what is outlined in the Warlock section and Find Familiar section)   
    - Companion Templates have  
      - Name, species, size, AC, HP, Speed  
      - Traits, features, actions  
      - Inventory (optional).  
      - Tags: Familiar, Mount, Pet, Automaton, etc.  
      - Companion Traits  
        - Defined like intrinsics but flagged as “Companion-only”; e.g., “Flyby,” “Bound Soul,” “Loyalty Bond”

- ## User Generated Content

  - Can view all user created content  
  - Can “promote” item to be in full database for all users   
  - Can mark as reviewed which hides it from the list 

- ## Manage users

  - View all users \+ metadata (email, join date, characters count, last login)  
  - Search \+ Filter users  
  - Disable/ban accounts  
  - Reset passwords manually  
  - Restore backups / imports  
  - View all custom created by a user  
  - Reassign ownership of characters or companions

- ## Import Data

  - Take an import from other sources to populate like 5e.tools

- ## Admin User Roles

  - Superadmin — all powers, can create other admins  
  - Content Admin — can manage feats, items, spells, species, etc.  
  - Moderator — can manage users, reset passwords  
  - Content Reviewer — can only view/approve user submissions

# Wizard / Workflows

# Wizards / Workflows 

## General Customization

* Add a custom action, spell, passive trait, or reaction manually  
* Define:  
  * Name, description,  
  * Usage limits (per rest, per day, per session),  
  * Recharge type (short rest, long rest, X/day, “session only”),  
  * Save DC or attack roll if applicable,  
  * Damage dice and type (optional),  
  * Tags (e.g., Bonus Action, Reaction, Spell, Skill, Intrinsic, Custom Origin, etc.)  
  * Skill modifiers

#### Where it appears

* Fully integrated into:  
  * Actions Tab (broken into sections like "Class Features", "Magic Items", "Custom"),  
  * Combat UI summary (if relevant),  
  * Spells Tab, if it's a spell-type ability,  
* Auto-tracked alongside rest logic:  
  * Usage counters reset on short/long rest as specified,  
  * Manual override always available.

#### Admin Tooling

* Admins can pre-build:  
  * Campaign-specific powers (e.g., Flame Step from a sidequest),  
  * Faction-based unlockables,  
  * World-specific boons and curses (like Val’Ruvina-specific magic or Leyfarer ranks),  
* Players can receive these via:  
  * Selectable lists,  
  * Or auto-assignment via companion features (in a future release).

## Character

* Character can be noted it as a companion / familiar which gives you any of the options defined in the admin  
* Species selection (with species-appropriate attribute traits).  
* Background  
* Origin  
* Stat allocation and hit points.  
* Class selection (core classes available)  
  * Sub Class Selection  
* Pull in languages, proficiencies, feats from the admin list.

## Leveling

* A "Level Up" modal will allow selecting which class gets the next level  
* Add feat at specified levels.  
* Unlock the abilities tied to the class level  
* Choose epic boon at level 19\.  
* Ability to level multiple levels at once (e.g., 4 at a time)  
* Select a second class at any point after level 1,  
  * See class breakdowns (e.g., “Fighter 3 / Wizard 2”) with a class-level summary view,  
  * Choose subclass(es) independently when available for each class.  
* We'll enforce multiclass requirements (e.g., minimum 13 in key abilities) with user prompts and soft warnings if not met.  
* Spellcasting and other scaling features (like Extra Attack, Feats, Proficiency Bonus) will be:  
  * Calculated according to 5e \+ Leyfarer multiclass rules,  
  * Explained with tooltips or toggles (especially for combined spellcasting rules)  
* Features that come from different classes will be grouped under collapsible sections per class.  
* All class-based spell sections will be maintained and togglable

## Custom Item

## Custom Intrinsics

## Custom Feat

## Behaviors

* offers smart suggestions based on rule logic (e.g., “You gain proficiency in two skills from this list…”),  
* allows the user to override, skip, or manually assign any value,  
* highlights missing or incomplete areas gently (e.g., “you haven’t selected your Fighting Style yet”).  
* any field can be re-edited manually  
* the app never blocks the player from progressing due to incomplete or "incorrect" setups  
* the system remembers what was done via wizard vs. manual override for traceability/debugging.

# UI/UX

# UI / UX

## User Interface

* Clean, minimal design for in-game use.  
* Responsive (desktop, mobile, tablet).  
* Mobile-first design as this is for using at the table

| Screens | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| **1\. Login & Account** | Auth \+ account settings |
| **2\. Character Select** | Avatar grid / list of owned characters |
| **3\. Character Sheet** | Primary tabbed view: Character, Combat, Spells, Inventory, Aptitudes |
| **4\. Level Up Wizard** | Step-by-step class/subclass/feat/aptitude |
| **5\. Rest Modal** | Short/Long/Session rest recovery UI |
| **6\. Export & Share** | PDF export \+ live link share |
| **7\. Admin Panel** | Edit and import content |

### Routing Structure

/                      		→ login (or redirect to /characters)  
/characters            	→ character select screen  
/characters/\[id\]       	→ character tab (root of character)  
/characters/\[id\]/\[tab\]   	→ character sheet (tabs: character, combat, etc.)  
/characters/\[id\]/level  	→ level up wizard  
/admin                 	→ admin panel  
/export/\[id\]           	→ printable/export view  
/share/\[linkId\]        	→ readonly sync viewer

### Component Tree

\<LayoutShell\>  
 ├─ \<Sidebar /\>              	 // Nav on desktop  
 ├─ \<TopBar /\> 	     	// Character info, avatar, settings  
      ├─ \<SettingsButton /\>                 
 ├─ \<TabBar /\>                	// Character| Combat | Spells | Inventory | Aptitudes | Breaks  
 └─ \<TabContent /\>            	// Switches by selected tab  
      ├─ \<CharacterTab /\>  
      ├─ \<CombatTab /\>  
      ├─ \<SpellsTab /\>  
      ├─ \<InventoryTab /\>  
      ├─ \<AptitudesTab /\>  
      ├─ \<BreaksTab /\>  
     

Each tab has **modular subcomponents**:

\<TopBar\>  
  ├─ \<Charater Name /\>  
  ├─ \<Avatar /\>  
  ├─ \<Level /\>  
  ├─ \<HeroicInspiration /\>  
  ├─ \<SettingsButton /\>  
\</TopBar\>

\<CharacterTab\>  
 ├─ \<AbilityScores /\>  
 ├─ \<ProficiencyBouns /\>  
 	├─ \<BaseProficiencyBouns /\>  
	├─ \<PassivePerception /\>  
	├─ \<SavingThrows /\>  
├─ \<Skills /\>  
├─ \<Aptitudes /\>  
├─ \<Species /\>  
 	├─ \<Species Info /\>  
 	├─ \<NonCombatTraits /\>  
├─ \<Class /\>  
 	├─ \<NonCombatClassFeatures /\>  
├─ \<Feat /\>  
 	├─ \<NonCombatFeats /\>  
 ├─ \<CompanionStatBlock /\>  
\</CharacterTab\>

\<CombatTab\>  
 ├─ \<Primary/\>	// Includes Armor Class, Initiative Bonus, and Walk Speed  
 ├─ \<HealthBlock /\>  // Toggle Health up and down  
	├─ \<DeathSavingThrows /\>	  
	├─ \<HealthBlock /\>  
	├─ \<ExhaustionPoints/\>	  
 ├─ \<Effects/\>  
 	├─ \<Concentration /\>  
 	├─ \<DamageResistances /\>  
 	├─ \<StatusEffects /\>  
 ├─ \<ActionList /\>	// Includes any combat Intrinsics  
 	├─ \<Weapons /\>  
	├─ \<SpellAttack /\>  
 	├─ \<SpellsDC/\>  
 	├─ \<Spells /\>  
 ├─ \<BonusActions /\>  
 	├─ \<Spells /\>  
 ├─ \<Reactions /\>  
 	├─ \<Spells /\>  
 ├─ \<CompanionCombatBlock /\>  
	 ├─ \<ActionList /\>  
 ├─ \<BonusActions /\>  
 ├─ \<Reactions /\>  
\</CombatTab\>

\<SpellTab\>			//A lot of the complexity is the actions to add/remove spells  
 ├─ \<Class /\>  
 	├─ \<Allowed /\>	// Qty to Prepare, Learn, Cantrips, Known, etc  
	├─ \<Update /\>	// Button to Prepare and/or Learn, Shows all available spells   
├─ \<Levels /\>  
 ├─ \<NonClassSpells /\>  
	├─ \<AddSpell /\>	// Show all spells for all classes separated by level  
├─ \<Aptitudes /\>	// If any Feat, Lineage, Trait, Item, etc. spell grants  
\</SpellTab\>

\<InventoryTab\>  
 ├─ \<Header/\>  
	├─ \<Gold /\>  
	├─ \<AddItem /\>  
	├─ \<Filters /\> // Is Consumable? Is Magic?  
 ├─ \<Attuned/\>  
 ├─ \<Equipped /\>  
 ├─ \<Backpack /\>  
 ├─ \<Stored /\>  
\</InventoryTab\>

\<AptitudesTab\>  
 ├─ \<Feats /\>  
 ├─ \<Languages /\>  
 ├─ \<Weapons /\>  
 ├─ \<Armor /\>  
 ├─ \<ToolsandInstruments /\>  
 ├─ \<Intrinsics /\>  
\</AptitudesTab\>

\<BreaksTab\>  
 ├─ \<ShortRest /\>  
 ├─ \<LongRest /\>  
 ├─ \<SessionRest /\>  
 ├─ \<LevelChange /\>  
\</BreaksTab\>

\<SettingsButton\>  
├─ \<SwitchCharater /\>   
├─ \<Charater /\>  
 	├─ \<UploadAvatar /\>  
 	├─ \<CharSettings /\>  
 	├─ \<Changelog /\>  
 	├─ \<Export /\>  
 	├─ \<Share /\>  
 	├─ \<DeleteChar /\>  
 ├─ \<AccountSettings /\>  
 	├─ \<Reset Password /\>  
 	├─ \<Update Email /\>  
 	├─ \<Feedback /\>  
 	├─ \<Links /\>   // Policy etc  
 	├─ \<Logout /\>  
 	├─ \<DeleteAccount /\>  
 \</SettingsButton\>

### State Handling 

| Layer | Location |
| :---- | :---- |
| Local UI (modals, toggles) | `useState` or `useReducer` inside components |
| Character data (spells, stats, items) | Redux slice: `characterData` |
| Server data (feats, items, spells) | Redux slice: `content` (or React Query) |
| Auth/session | Redux slice: `auth` |
| Sync/live share | Redux slice: `sync` |
| Settings/preferences | Redux slice: `ui` (persisted via localStorage) |

### Reusables

\<StatBox /\> \- editable value with calc \+ override  
\<ToggleIcon /\> \-  advantage/disadvantage switch  
	\<On /\>  
	\<Off /\>  
\<QTYToggle /\>  
\<X /\> \- removed line / close modal  
\<InfoModal /\>  
\<RestSummaryModal /\>  
\<SpellCard /\>  
\<ValueCalcModal /\>  
\<ItemRow /\>  
\<Tabs /\> \- Has a collection of Sections  
\<StaticSection /\>  
\<ExpandableSection /\> \- collapsible group wrapper  
	\<OpenSection /\>  
	\<ClosedSection /\>  
\<EditableField /\> — inline override support

# Technical Design

# Overview

| Layer | Tech | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Frontend** | React \+ [Next.js](http://Next.js) (?) |  |
| **Backend** | Node.js \+ Express (or NestJS) (?) | Modular API service |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL (?) | Relational, well-suited for structured game data |
| **Realtime** | Socket.IO or Supabase Realtime (?) | For live link sharing / syncing |
| **Auth** | Supabase Auth or Firebase Auth (?) | Supports Google OAuth \+ email/password |
| **Storage** | S3-compatible (or Supabase storage) (?) | For avatars, PDFs, exports |
| **PDF Generation** | Puppeteer or PDFKit (?) | Export custom / official-style sheets |
| **Admin Panel** | React \+ [Next.js](http://Next.js) (?) |  |

# General 

- ## Global State Management

- Offline is key and should be handled well, this will require state tracking etc.   
- Organize state by domain slices, separating  
  - Persistent synced data (characters, spells)  
  - UI state (modals, panel toggles)  
  - Live overrides and local edits.

- ## LocalStorage / Cache Strategy

  - Persisted slices: auth, ui, content (optional), last loaded character  
  - Unsaved edits tracked via overrides or draft buffer  
  - Offline-first rendering with autosave queue  
  - Warn if Offline and Online version have Divergence

## Authentication

* User registration and login via Google OAuth  
* Manual user registration  
  * Create account screen  
  * User Name, Password, Email  
  * Hold screen that Account needs to be validated  
  * Send email to validate account  
  * Active account after validation  
* Account Management  
  * Forgot Password  
  * Forgot Username

# Calculations

* App handles all calculations automatically.  
* Manual override available to account for house rules or DM exceptions.  
* Toggle effects to update character sheet in real-time.  
* Temporary \+ override modifiers applied from user should show a small asterisk

# Definitions 

| Field | Required? | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Name | ✅ | Unique, user-facing |
| Description / Lore | ✅ | Markdown-enabled |
| Tags | Optional | For filtering, source flagging (TLC, SRD, PHB2024) |
| Source | ✅ | e.g., "PHB", "TLC", "Homebrew" |
| Prerequisites | Optional | Level, species, class, feat, lineage, etc. |
| Effects | ✅ | AC bonus, spell slots, languages, damage mod, etc. |
| Charge/Regen Logic | Optional | d6/day, once/session, long rest, manual |
| Overrides Allowed | ✅ | Yes/no flag |
| Visibility/Availability | ✅ | Public, Locked (DM only), Faction Unlocked, Hidden |

### 

# Enum Types

| Type | Examples |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Language** | Common, Deep Speech, Val’Ruvian |
| **Tool** | Calligrapher’s Supplies, Carpenter’s Tools, Cartographer’s Tools, Cobbler’s Tools, Cook’s Utensils, Glassblower’s Tools, Jeweler’s Tools, Leatherworker’s Tools, Mason’s Tools, Painter’s Supplies, Potter’s Tools, Smith’s Tools, Tinker’s Tools, Weaver’s Tools, Woodcarver’s Tools, Disguise Kit, Forgery Kit, dice, dragonchess, playing cards, three-dragon ante, Herbalism Kit, Navigator’s Tools, Thieves’ Tools |
| **Instrument** | Bagpipes, Drum, Dulcimer, Flute, Horn, Lute, Lyre, Pan flute, Shawm, Violin |
| **Weapon** | Simple, Martial |
| **Armor** | Light, Medium, Heavy, Shields |
| **Damage / Resistance** | Acid, Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, Necrotic, Piercing, Poison, Psychic, Radiant, Slashing, Thunder |
| **Item** | Weapon, Armor, Consumable, Wondrous, Tool, Instrument, Component, Misc |
| **Attributes / Saves** | Strength, Constitution, Dexterity, Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma |
| **Skills** | Acrobatics, Animal Handling, Arcana, Athletics, Deception, History, Insight, Intimidation, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Performance, Persuasion, Religion, Sleight of Hand, Stealth, Survival |
| **Spell Schools** | Abjuration, Conjuration, Divination, Enchantment, Evocation, Illusion, Necromancy, Transmutation |
| **Weapon Mastery** | Cleave, Graze, Nick, Push, Sap, Slow, Topple, Vex |
| **Creature Type** | Aberration, Beast, Celestial, Construct, Dragon, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, Giant, Humanoid, Monstrosity, Ooze, Plant, Undead |
| **Bonus** | Proficiency Bonus, Ability Check Bonus, Attack Roll Bonus, Damage Roll Bonus, Saving Throw Bonus, Spell Attack Bonus, Spell Save DC Bonus, Ability Score Bonus,  |
| **Activations** | Action, Bonus Action, Reaction, X time, Free Action |
| **Reset Conditions** | Short Rest, Long Rest, Session Rest, Custom |
| **Feats** | Origin, General, Fighting Style, Epic Boon |

# Effects

* 

# Database Design

# Database Architecture

## Spells

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Name** | Text | Unique spell name |
| **Level** | Integer (0–9) | 0 \= Cantrip |
| **School** | Enum | Abjuration, Conjuration, etc. |
| **Casting Time** | Dropdown/Text | “1 Action”, “1 Bonus Action”, “1 Minute”, etc. |
| **Range** | Text | Structured text (e.g., “30 ft”, “Touch”, “Self”) |
| **Duration** | Text | Includes special handling: Concentration, Until Dispelled, etc. |
| **Components** | Checkboxes | V / S / M, with optional material note |
| **Concentration** | Boolean | Toggles duration handling |
| **Ritual** | Boolean | Toggles ritual-castable flag |
| **Description** | Markdown | Full rules text |
| **Higher Levels Text** | Markdown | Optional override for upcasting rules |
| **Source** | Dropdown | PHB, TCE, TLC, Custom |
| **Visibility** | Enum | Public / Locked / Hidden |
| **Tags** | Chips | e.g., Healing, Damage, Control, TLC-only |
| **Custom/Official** | Boolean | For filtering |
| **Damage Dice** | Text | e.g., “2d6” |
| **Damage Type** | Enum | Fire, Necrotic, Force, etc. |
| **Save Type** | Enum | CON, DEX, WIS, etc. (if any) |
| **Attack Roll** | Boolean | True \= spell requires to-hit roll |
| **Affects** | Multi-select | Creatures, Objects, Area, etc. |
| **Conditions Applied** | Tags | e.g., Restrained, Frightened |
| **Applies Status Effect** | Enum/Text | Decrease Con, etc |
| **Combat/Non** | Boolean | Does it display on combat tab or in Character tab |

## Items

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Name** | Text | Unique item name |
| **Type** | Dropdown | Weapon, Armor, Consumable, Wondrous, Tool, Instrument, Component, Misc |
| **Is Magical** | Boolean | Look at the magical effect table |
| **Is Consumable** | Boolean | Is the item consumable |
| **Description** | Markdown | Narrative \+ mechanical text |
| **Source** | Dropdown | PHB, TLC, Custom |
| **Visibility** | Enum | Public / Locked / Hidden |
| **Tags** | Chips | e.g., “Fire”, “Healing”, “Summoning”, “TLC-only” |
| **Custom/Official** | Boolean | Filter & promotion logic |
| **Weight** | Float | In pounds |
| **Gold Value** | Float | Decimal supports 0.1 (silver), 0.01 (copper) |
| **Stackable** | Boolean | Default true unless unique, magical, or equippable |

### Magic Specific

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Rarity** | Enum | Common, Uncommon, Rare, Very Rare, Legendary, Unique (adds tooltip coloring) |
| **Is Cursed** | Boolean | Is the item cursed |
| **Attunement Required** | Boolean | Triggers attunement system logic |
| **Total Charges** | Numeric |  |
| **Charge Reset** | Enum | Short Rest, Long Rest, Session Rest, Custom |

### Weapon Specific

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Damage Dice** | Text | e.g., 1d8 |
| **Damage Type** | Enum | Slashing, Piercing, etc. |
| **Range** | Text | e.g., “Melee”, “30/120” |
| **Properties** | Multi-select | e.g., Finesse, Reach, Thrown |
| **Weapon Mastery** | Enum | Nick, Cleave, etc. |
| **Bonus to Hit/Damage** | Integer | e.g., \+1 from magic sword |
| **Requires Ammo Type** | Optional | (e.g., Arrows, Bolts) |

### Armor Specific

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Base AC** | Integer | e.g., 14 |
| **Dex Cap** | Optional | e.g., “+2 max” |
| **Strength Requirement** | Optional | e.g., 13 |
| **Stealth Disadvantage** | Boolean | Yes/No |
| **Armor Type** | Enum | Light / Medium / Heavy / Shield |

## Aptitudes

### Proficiency

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Name** | Text | e.g., “Tinker’s Tools”, “Simple Weapons”, “Deep Speech” |
| **Type** | Enum | Language / Tool and Instrument / Weapon / Armor |
| **Description** | Optional | Markdown — use for flavor or rule-specific notes |
| **Tags** | Chips | e.g., TLC-only, Exotic, Legacy |
| **Source** | Dropdown | PHB, TLC, Custom |
| **Visibility** | Enum | Public / Locked / Hidden |
| **Custom/Official** | Boolean | Promotion filter |

### Feats

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Name** | Text | Unique name |
| **Description** | Markdown | Rules text and narrative |
| **Source** | Dropdown | e.g., PHB, TCE, TLC, Custom |
| **Visibility** | Enum | Public / Locked / Hidden |
| **Level Requirement** | Integer (optional) | Optional for feats gated by level |
| **Prerequisites** | Text | Freeform or structured fields (e.g., Species: Elf; Class: Rogue)?  |
| **Tags** | Chips | e.g., Combat, Utility, Spellcasting, TLC-only |
| **Custom/Official** | Boolean | For filtering and promotion controls |
| **Combat/Non** | Boolean | Does it display on combat tab or in Character tab |

### Intrinsics

### Skills

### CharaterStats

### Species / Lineages / Traits

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Name** | Text | e.g., Elf, Frogfolk, Gnome |
| **Subtypes / Lineages** | List of entries | e.g., Moon Elf, Sun Elf, Shadow Elf |
| **Description** | Markdown | Lore and common physical/cultural traits |
| **Source** | Dropdown | PHB, TLC, Custom |
| **Visibility** | Enum | Public / Locked / Hidden |
| **Tags** | Chips | e.g., Fey, Wilderfolk, Custom, Tiny-only |
| **Creature Type** | Enum | Humanoid, Fey, Beast, etc. |
| **Size Options** | Multi-select | Small, Medium (some species choose at creation) |
| **Speed** | Integer | Default walking speed (in feet) |
| **Darkvision** | Boolean \+ range | e.g., 60 ft |
| **Innate Traits** | List of traits | Always granted to all characters of this species |
| **Selectable Traits** | List of traits | Player chooses N traits from list (configurable) |
| **Languages Known** | List | Starting languages (plus Common) |

#### Lineage

#### Traits

## Classes

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Class Name** | Text | e.g., Fighter, Fateweaver |
| **Source** | Dropdown | PHB, TLC, Custom |
| **Hit Die** | Enum | d6 / d8 / d10 / d12 |
| **Primary Ability** | Multi-select | Used for multiclass prerequisites |
| **Proficiencies Gained** | Structured | Armor, Weapons, Tools, Saving Throws |
| **Skills Available** | List \+ number to choose | e.g., “Choose 2 from \[list\]” |
| **Spellcasting** | Object | Includes level acquired, progression table, prep/known mechanic |
| **Subclass Level** | Integer | Level at which subclass is chosen |
| **Visibility** | Enum | Public / Locked / Hidden |
| **Tags** | Chips | e.g., “Full Caster”, “Half Caster”, “Melee”, “TLC” |
| **Level Features** | Table | 1–20 feature list, described below |
| **Weapon Mastery** | Enum \+ Count | Supports 2024 Mastery rules (e.g., “2 at level 1”) |

### Level Features

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Combat/Non** | Boolean | Does it display on combat tab or in Character tab |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |

### Level Gated Choice

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Choice Label** | Text | What the user sees (“Choose your Divine Order”) |
| **Options** | List of objects | Each option contains a name, description, and effects |
| **Grants** | Structured | Same as feat fields — spells, proficiencies, actions, modifiers |
| **UI Behavior** | Enum | Dropdown, Cards, Radio Buttons (affects presentation in app) |
| **Optional?** | Boolean | e.g., if some classes get to skip or defer a choice |

Choice lives on the Character Table

Examples

Cleric: Choose a Divine Order at level 1 (e.g., Protector, Thaumaturge, Scholar)

Fighter: Choose a Combat Style at level 1

Druid (TLC): Choose a Dreamwild Bond

Warlock (TLC): Choose a Pact Mutation

| Field | Type | Applies To | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Associated Skill** | Enum | Tool/Instrument | e.g., Sleight of Hand for Forgery Kit |
| **Mastery Required** | Boolean | Weapon/Armor | Enables control over who can use the proficiency effectively |
| **Rarity** | Enum | Language | Common, Rare |

## Character 

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| `id` | UUID | Primary key |
| `owner_id` | UUID | Foreign key to users table |
| `name` | Text | Character name |
| `avatar_url` | String | Optional |
| `metadata` | JSONB | Stores custom UI prefs, tags, etc. |
| `is_companion` | Boolean | Toggles limited view |
| `companion_of` | UUID (nullable) | If companion, links to main character |

## User

| Table | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| `users` | Registered players/admins |
| `user_settings` | Encumbrance toggle, default avatar, etc. |
| `user_characters` | All character ownership relationships |
| `user_roles` | Admin/mod/content status |

# Effects

# Join Tables

# Questions

At what point should everything be structured vs thrown into a JSON file to be parased in the DB?

E.G. with level 1 cleric they are forced to choose between two options for their divine order feature

Do we have a choices table that references the level gated choice table which is referenced by the level features table OR do we put it into a JSON blob on the level gated choices table that looks like

{  
  "name": "Protector",  
  "description": "Trained for battle, you gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Heavy armor.",  
  "grants": {  
    "proficiency": \[PROF123,PROF124\],  
  }  
}  
{  
  "name": "Thaumaturge",  
  "description": "You know one extra cantrip from the Cleric spell list. In addition, your mystical connection to the divine gives you a bonus to your Intelligence (Arcana or Religion) checks. The bonus equals your Wisdom modifier (minimum of \+1).",  
  "grants": {  
    "proficiency": \[PROF122,PROF111\],  
	"bonus": \+wis,  
	"spells" {  
		"cantrip": \+1  
		}  
		}  
  }  
}

# Future

# Future

* ### Encumbrance

  * It is out of scope for this app; the notes that apply to it have been added here  
  * Enable / Disable encumbrance in options  
  * Backpack — does not affect stats but contributes to weight  
    * Subsection of “Used” for consumables with a 0 qty  
  * Stored: (e.g., bank, cart, home) does not contribute to total weight if Encumbrance is enabled.  
  * Weight total   
    * Show only If Encumbrance is enabled  
    * An attribute for magic items and skills should have the ability to effect how much a character can carry (e.g Powerful Build and can double (count as a size larger for) their push/lift/carry)   
    * If enabled, Equipped and Backpack items count toward weight,  
      Stored items are excluded 

**Other Features to be added** 

- Option to add a custom Intrinsic / Feat  
- with an option to create a custom Companion types

[image9]: <data:image/png;base64,>
